codeReview.summary: This fix commit resolves four of the five targeted backend scrutiny findings: HookType key enforcement in plugin_registry, defensive hook_type logging/type guards in plugin_hooks, chat-start fallback restoration in chat_graph_runner, and method-context coverage in node_provider_runtime errors. However, the route-id collision issue from backend-core-decoupling is not fully resolved in webhook route resolution, and the updated collision handling leaves stale ownership state that can remove the active route mapping when an older agent is deleted.

issues.description: resolve_node_route_by_id still resolves duplicate route IDs by lexicographically sorting (node_type, route_id) keys and taking the first entry. If multiple node types share the same route_id, webhook dispatch can still target an unintended node type; this leaves the prior blocking ambiguity from backend-core-decoupling unresolved.

During same-key overwrite ((node_type, route_id) collision), _ROUTES_BY_AGENT is not rebalanced for the previous owner. Later remove_agent_routes(old_agent) pops that shared key from _ROUTE_INDEX and deletes the active last-write-wins mapping. This creates route loss/404 behavior after agent removal and is a correctness regression in collision handling.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state does not document a canonical webhook route-id uniqueness contract across node types. This ambiguity appears to be driving repeated fixes in node_route_index without a stable invariant for resolve_node_route_by_id.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/services/node_route_index.py resolves by route_id alone via _ROUTE_ID_INDEX + sorted key selection (lines 58-62), while mission AGENTS/library docs do not define whether route_id must be globally unique or namespaced by node_type.

summary: FAIL — commit 9f2237a fixes most prior backend scrutiny blockers, but node_route_index still has blocking route-id correctness issues: unresolved cross-type route-id ambiguity in resolve_node_route_by_id and stale ownership behavior that can drop active routes after agent removal.
